title: lagerstroemia paniculata
intro: Lagerstroemia paniculata, also known as the Indian crape myrtle or crepe myrtle, is a deciduous shrub native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and soothing digestive issues. Lagerstroemia paniculata can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to treat respiratory and gastrointestinal problems.
